data.title: Euro Area GDP expands 1.4% Y/Y in Q3
data.description:
The GDP  In the Euro Area expanded 0.30% in Q3 2025 over the previous quarter, above estimates of 0.2%.

Y/Y Eurozone GDP grew 1.4%, compared to the 1.6% expansion recorded in both the first and second quarters, and broadly in line with the previous estimate. 

Spain led the major economies, expanding 2.8% (vs 3% in Q2), followed by the Netherlands at 1.6% (vs 1.7%) and France at 0.9% (up from 0.7%). 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Tesla rolls out low-cost Model 3 variant in Europe
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) on Friday launched a new lower-priced version of its Model 3 in Europe, a few months after rolling it out in the U.S., Reuters reported.

The Model 3 Standard is intended to help revive demand as Tesla faces intensifying competition from European and Chinese EV makers offering more affordable models.

Pricing for the new Model 3 Standard is set at €37,970 ($44,256) in Germany and 449,990 Swedish crowns ($47,849) in Sweden, according to the company’s website, while the model currently sells for $36,990 in the U.S.

The move follows the October launch of a lower-priced Model Y crossover in Europe.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) were up 0.22% premarket at $455.32.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: HPE, RBRK, SOFI, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher in the premarket hours of Friday as investors positioned ahead of the delayed September PCE inflation report, a pivotal indicator for the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision on December 10.​

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Rubrik (RBRK) +17% - Shares jumped after the company posted a strong Q3, with revenue up 48% Y/Y and subscription revenue up 52% Y/Y. Free cash flow came in at $76.9M, sharply higher than $15.6M a year ago. For Q4, the company expects revenue of $341M–$343M, ahead of the ~$325M estimate. Rubrik also raised its FY2026 outlook, guiding revenue to $1.28B (vs. prior $1.23B and in line with estimates) and boosting its free cash flow forecast to $194M–$202M from $145M–$155M.
  • ChargePoint (CHPT) +12% – Shares rallied after the EV-charging company delivered stronger-than-expected results, with revenue up 6% Y/Y to $106M, topping the high end of its guidance range. Subscription revenue rose 15% to $42M, while GAAP gross margin improved to 31% and non-GAAP margin held at a record 33%. The company also highlighted a post-quarter debt reduction of $172M, cutting its total by more than half. For FQ4, ChargePoint guided revenue to $100M–$110M, with the $105M midpoint coming in ahead of consensus expectations of $102.89M.
  • Ulta Beauty (ULTA) +6% – Shares rose after the retailer delivered a standout Q3, posting strong beats on both revenue and earnings along with comp-store growth that nearly doubled expectations. Reflecting the robust quarter and confidence in a strong holiday season, the company raised its full-year outlook. Ulta now expects sales of roughly $12.3B, above prior guidance and ahead of the consensus of $12.06B, with comps now projected to rise 4.4%–4.7%. EPS guidance was lifted to $25.20–$25.50 from the prior $23.85 and $24.30, topping estimates of $24.57, and operating margin expectations were also raised to 12.3%–12.4% from 11.9%–12.0%.

Biggest stock losers

  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) -9% - Shares dropped sharply after the company delivered mixed fiscal Q4 results and issued guidance that fell short of several Street expectations. For FQ1, HPE projected adjusted EPS of $0.57–$0.61, topping the $0.53 consensus, but guided revenue to $9B–$9.4B, well under the anticipated $9.87B. Full-year adjusted EPS is now expected to come in at $2.25–$2.45, with the midpoint just below estimates, while free cash flow of $1.7B–$2B landed meaningfully under the $2.2B forecast.
  • SentinelOne (S) -8% – Despite the Q3 beat, shares fell as the company issued a softer-than-expected Q4 revenue outlook, guiding to $271M vs. the Street’s $271.89M estimate. FY2025 revenue is projected at $1.001B, broadly in line with expectations. The update came alongside news that CFO Barbara Larson will step down in mid-January 2026, with Chief Growth Officer Barry Padgett set to assume the interim CFO role.
  • SoFi Technologies (SOFI) -6% – Shares slipped after the company priced its public offering of 54.5M shares at $27.50, raising an expected ~$1.5B. Underwriters have a 30-day option to buy an additional ~8.2M shares. The deal is slated to close on December 8, 2025, with proceeds earmarked for general corporate purposes, strengthening capital, and supporting future growth initiatives.
  • DocuSign (DOCU) -6% - Shares slid even though the company posted Q3 results and guidance that topped expectations. For Q4, it expects revenue of $825M–$829M, with the $827M midpoint just shy of the ~$827.4M consensus. Subscription revenue is projected at $808M–$812M, and billings at $992M–$1B. Adjusted gross margin is seen at 80.8%–81.1%. The company also raised its full-year outlook, now guiding revenue to $3.208B–$3.212B, above its prior $3.19B–$3.2B range. Subscription revenue is expected at $3.14B–$3.144B, with FY billings forecast at $3.38B–$3.39B. Adjusted gross margin is now seen at 81.7%–81.8%, and adjusted operating margin at 29.8%–29.9%.

data.title: Cloudflare fixes outage that hit banking sites, major apps
data.description:
Cloudflare (NET) said it has resolved an issue that caused outages across the websites of several banks, along with major platforms, including Shopify, Zoom, and LinkedIn, on Friday.

The company confirmed on its status page that it had “implemented a fix” after earlier disruptions affected its dashboard and related APIs.

“A change made to how Cloudflare's Web Application Firewall parses requests caused Cloudflare's network to be unavailable for several minutes this morning. This was not an attack; the change was deployed by our team to help mitigate the industry-wide vulnerability disclosed this week in React Server Components. We will share more information as we have it today,” the company added.

Cloudflare (NET) shares fell as much as 6% in premarket trading before trimming losses to about 2.5%. The outage followed a major disruption last month that temporarily knocked out services, including X and ChatGPT.

data.title: Sean Duffy: New fuel economy rule could bring back the '70s station wagon
data.description:
The Trump administration's proposal to slash Biden-era fuel economy standards could bring back the once popular station wagons, Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy told CNBC.

"This rule will actually allow you to bring back the 1970s station wagon - maybe a little wood paneling on the side," Duffy said. "We can bring back choice to consumers. So yeah, the minivan is awesome, but maybe the station wagon is cool too."

The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has proposed slashing fuel economy requirements, which it said led automakers to "reshape the market in unanticipated ways - such as by almost eliminating the production of station wagons in favor of vehicles like minivans and crossover utility vehicles."

This is because minivans and SUVs were classified as light trucks, meaning they were subject to less stringent standards.

Station wagons used to be a staple for families in the U.S., but their popularity was dented by higher fuel prices and new emission standards in the '70s, as well as the emergence of minivans in the '80s.

Mini-cars

Duffy said he is working with the NHTSA on "clearing the deck" for U.S. manufacturing of mini-cars.

"If there's a market for those vehicles, I want to give our manufacturers the opportunity to build those cars," Duffy said, as Trump directed him to change the rules to bring mini-cars to the U.S.

"Are they going to work on the freeways? Probably not," Duffy said, adding that it could be a "great solution" for cities and a much more affordable option.

Mini-cars are especially popular in Japan, where they're called kei cars. But these cars don't meet federal standards for speed and safety in the U.S.

"They're very small. They're really cute," Trump said this week. "But we're not allowed to make them in this country and I think you're gonna do very well with those cars, so we're gonna approve those cars."

data.title: Fluidstack in talks for $700M funding at $7B valuation - report
data.description:
Fluidstack is looking to raise around $700 million in a funding round that would value the cloud-computing startup at $7 billion, Bloomberg reported, citing familiar with the situation.

Situational Awareness, a firm started by ex-OpenAI researcher Leopold Aschenbrenner, is in talks to lead the round, with Alphabet's Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) also involved.

Fluidstack, a UK-registered startup that recently relocated its global headquarters to New York, is part of the emerging “neocloud” sector that rents computing power for AI development. This year, the company struck two debt-financed data center deals with crypto miners TeraWulf (WULF) and Cipher Mining (CIFR), with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) acting as the financial backstop.

The company was also featured in French President Emmanuel Macron’s AI push, announcing plans in February to build a €10 billion ($11.5 billion), 1-gigawatt supercomputer in France by 2026, and lists partners including Meta (META), Honeywell (HON), and multiple AI startups.

data.title: ASSA ABLOY buys Sargent and Greenleaf in the U.S., expects immediate EPS accretion
Canadian Natural Resources prices C$1.65B notes
data.description:
• Canadian Natural Resources Limited (CNQ) priced C$1.65B in medium-term notes consisting of three tranches sold to Canadian investors.
  • The notes consists of C$550M 3.30% coupon 3-year note at C$99.887 with 3.340% YTM; C$550M 3.75% coupon 5-year note at C$99.781 with 3.798% YTM; and C$550M 4.45% coupon 10-year note at C$99.700 with 4.588% YTM.
  • The net proceeds received by Canadian Natural from the issuance and sale of the Notes will be used for general corporate purposes. 
  • The offering is targeted to close on December 8, 2025.

data.title: Stock index futures rise ahead of inflation guage
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Friday, as traders looked forward to fresh inflation data in hopes of gauging the Federal Reserve's monetary policy. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.4% and Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

"Markets saw a modest risk-on move yesterday, as a decent batch of US data saw investors price in fewer rate cuts next year," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said. 

The personal income and outlays will land during market hours. Income and spending are both expected to be up a robust 0.4% on the month. PCE prices are seen up 0.3% on the month for total and core. 

The consumer sentiment report will also be released at the same time. 52.0 is expected for the first read in December, versus an abysmal 51.0 in the November final.
